claim: The US open-source AI business model is fundamentally broken because companies cannot profitably invest in model training when anyone else can immediately take the weights and serve inference at lower cost.
actionable: If building open-source AI in the US, plan for alternative revenue streams beyond inference serving (e.g., hosted solutions, vertical specialization, hardware bundling) rather than relying on pure inference margins.

claim: China's government subsidizes open-source AI to kill margins for competitors, a viable strategy because being first-mover advantage matters less than cost when quality is comparable across frontier and non-frontier models.
actionable: Evaluate open-source model choices by comparing total cost of ownership and control rather than frontier capability benchmarks, as most enterprise use cases do not need cutting-edge reasoning.

claim: If US enterprises standardize on Chinese open-source models, China gains influence over AI infrastructure, chip optimization directions, and potentially cultural content through subtle model biases that may not be fully removable.
actionable: When adopting open-source models, assess geopolitical and long-term control implications alongside performance and cost.

claim: Most US enterprises do not solve frontier math problems and need only 99% of use cases, making DeepSeek models at 1/10th the cost of OpenAI/Anthropic a rational choice despite lower frontier reasoning capability.
actionable: For enterprise AI adoption outside research/science, evaluate whether frontier reasoning capability (e.g., GPT-5 Pro vs DeepSeek) is required for your use case before committing to expensive closed models.

claim: Nvidia's position as hardware supplier lets them profitably open-source models because they benefit from increased compute demand regardless of who serves inference, unlike startups facing direct margin competition.
actionable: Consider hardware-aligned business models (selling inference via your own chips or partnering with hardware vendors) as a more sustainable path for open-source AI than pure software inference.
